metadata.subject: Physical and Health Education
metadata.description: Active Living 12 deepens student capability to lead, teach, and advocate for physical activity participation in their communities. Students develop advanced coaching and mentoring skills, design inclusive activity programs for diverse groups, and explore lifetime activity planning beyond high school. The course emphasizes peer leadership, community wellness contribution, and understanding how to support others in developing sustainable physical activity habits. Students engage with First Peoples perspectives on community health, collective wellness, and the role of movement in maintaining cultural practices and community connection.
metadata.general_description: An advanced leadership course developing coaching capability, community health advocacy, and the capacity to guide others toward lifelong physical activity participation and wellness.

big_ideas.statement: Leadership in physical activity begins with deep personal commitment and extends to meaningfully supporting others' wellness journeys.
big_ideas.elaboration: Grade 12 students develop the capacity to guide, mentor, and inspire peers and community members in physical activity, grounded in their own sustained commitment to movement.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding diverse needs, abilities, and identities enables creation of truly inclusive physical activity environments.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn to recognize and address barriers that prevent people of different abilities, ages, backgrounds, and identities from participating fully in physical activity.

big_ideas.statement: Coaching and mentoring skills translate personal fitness knowledge into meaningful support for others' development.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop practical abilities to assess others' needs, provide constructive feedback, demonstrate techniques safely, and create psychologically safe learning environments.

big_ideas.statement: Community-based physical activity programs contribute to population health and social cohesion.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students understand how schools, recreation departments, and community organizations create opportunities for physical activity, and how they can contribute to these efforts.

big_ideas.statement: First Peoples perspectives on collective wellness, land connection, and cultural movement practices inform authentic community health leadership.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students engage with Indigenous models of wellness that emphasize community, reciprocity, and the healing power of land-based and cultural practices.

categories.name: Coaching and Peer Leadership
categories.raw: Developing the skills to guide, teach, and mentor others in physical activity and fitness.

competencies.raw: Plan and lead activity sessions; demonstrate and teach movement skills; provide constructive feedback; adapt teaching for different learners; create inclusive and safe learning environments.
i_can_statements: I can plan and lead physical activity sessions that are engaging, appropriately challenging, and aligned with learning objectives.
I can clearly demonstrate movement techniques and explain key coaching cues that help learners understand and execute skills.
I can assess learner performance, identify technical issues or misconceptions, and provide specific, actionable feedback.
I can adapt teaching methods, activity complexity, and communication style to support diverse learners — including those with different abilities, learning styles, and confidence levels.
I can create physically and psychologically safe learning environments where all participants feel respected, valued, and encouraged to challenge themselves.

categories.name: Program Design and Community Leadership
categories.raw: Designing inclusive activity programs and contributing to community health and wellness.

competencies.raw: Design programs for diverse groups; understand community physical activity needs and assets; collaborate with community partners; advocate for physical activity access and equity.
i_can_statements: I can design inclusive physical activity programs that welcome and engage people of different ages, abilities, backgrounds, and identities.
I can assess community physical activity needs, identify barriers to participation, and design programs that address these barriers.
I can identify and engage community resources — facilities, organizations, volunteers, funding — that support physical activity programs.
I can advocate for policies and conditions that increase physical activity access and opportunity for all community members.
I can evaluate programs for effectiveness and make adjustments based on participant feedback and observed outcomes.

categories.name: Inclusive and Adaptive Fitness Leadership
categories.raw: Supporting physical activity participation for people of all abilities and creating accessible movement experiences.

competencies.raw: Understand barriers to physical activity participation; adapt activities and environments for accessibility; support people with different abilities and health conditions; promote equity in physical activity.
i_can_statements: I can identify and articulate barriers that prevent people from participating in physical activity — including social, economic, accessibility, and systemic barriers.
I can modify activities, environments, and instruction to ensure people of all abilities can participate meaningfully.
I can work supportively with people who have different physical abilities, chronic health conditions, mental health challenges, or other factors affecting activity.
I can examine how gender, race, socioeconomic status, and other identity factors affect physical activity access and experience.
I can advocate for and implement practical changes that increase physical activity equity and inclusion in schools and communities.

categories.name: Lifetime Physical Activity Planning and Community Contribution
categories.raw: Supporting others in developing lifelong activity engagement and contributing to community wellness.

competencies.raw: Support others in lifetime activity planning; contribute to school and community physical activity initiatives; understand career pathways in physical activity and wellness; model sustained personal commitment.
i_can_statements: I can guide peers in assessing their activity interests, setting realistic goals, and developing sustainable long-term plans for physical activity.
I can actively contribute to school physical activity initiatives — whether through program design, leadership, coaching, or advocacy.
I can engage with community physical activity programs and organizations, and contribute meaningfully to their missions.
I can articulate my own sustained commitment to physical activity and explain how it shapes my identity and choices.
I can explore careers in physical activity leadership — coaching, recreation, fitness instruction, athletic training, and health promotion.

areas.name: Coaching and Teaching Methods
areas.raw: Practical approaches to planning, teaching, and mentoring physical activities effectively.
topics: Lesson and session planning: setting learning objectives, sequencing activities, managing time, and evaluating outcomes
Demonstration and explanation: clearly teaching movement techniques using appropriate cues, models, and feedback strategies
Constructive feedback: providing specific, actionable, and encouraging feedback to support skill development
Differentiation and adaptation: modifying activities and instruction to support learners with different abilities, learning styles, and confidence
Group management: organizing participants, maintaining safety, and creating inclusive group dynamics
First Peoples teaching perspectives: incorporating cultural ways of knowing, emphasizing relationships and collective learning

areas.name: Program Design for Diverse Groups
areas.raw: Creating physical activity programs responsive to community needs and participant diversity.
topics: Needs assessment: understanding target community demographics, activity preferences, barriers, and existing assets
Inclusive program design: ensuring programs welcome and serve people of different ages, abilities, backgrounds, and identities
Accessibility and accommodation: designing physical and social environments that enable full participation
Community partnership and collaboration: engaging schools, recreation centers, clinics, and community organizations
Program evaluation: gathering feedback, tracking outcomes, and making continuous improvements
Funding and sustainability: understanding funding sources, budgeting, and maintaining long-term programs

areas.name: Supporting Physical Activity for People with Different Abilities
areas.raw: Approaches to inclusive and adaptive activity leadership for diverse participants.
topics: Common modifications: adapting exercises, activities, and environments for people with mobility, sensory, cognitive, or health differences
Chronic disease and physical activity: understanding how common health conditions affect activity participation and appropriate modifications
Mental health and physical activity: recognizing mental health impacts on activity, and using movement as a mental health support
Adaptive equipment and technology: using assistive devices, adaptive equipment, and technology to enable participation
Accessibility standards and legislation: understanding accessibility requirements and universal design principles
Peer support and inclusion: fostering an inclusive culture where all participants are valued and supported

areas.name: Community Health Advocacy and Physical Activity Systems
areas.raw: Understanding community health structures and advocating for physical activity access and equity.
topics: School physical activity systems: understanding school programs, policies affecting activity, and opportunities for student leadership
Community recreation infrastructure: exploring parks, recreation centers, sports facilities, and community organizations
Public health approaches: understanding how public health agencies promote population physical activity and health
Health equity and disparities: understanding how physical activity access differs across communities and populations
Advocacy strategies: using evidence, data, and communication to advocate for policy and environmental changes
Career pathways: understanding careers in recreation management, public health, fitness instruction, coaching, and health promotion

areas.name: Lifetime Active Living Planning and Personal Leadership
areas.raw: Supporting self and peers in sustainable lifelong activity engagement.
topics: Adult physical activity patterns: understanding how adult life circumstances, roles, and transitions affect activity participation
Sustaining motivation across lifespan: building habit, finding community, adapting to life changes, and maintaining lifelong commitment
Activity across cultures: exploring how different communities and cultures engage with physical activity and movement
First Peoples holistic wellness: understanding Indigenous approaches to health that center community, land, and cultural practices
Personal identity as an active person: articulating how physical activity shapes sense of self and life trajectory
Mentoring and modeling: demonstrating sustained commitment and inspiring others through personal example

areas.name: Diversity, Inclusion, and Social Justice in Physical Activity
areas.raw: Examining barriers and advancing equitable physical activity participation.
topics: Systemic barriers: understanding how poverty, racism, gender discrimination, disability discrimination, and other systemic factors affect activity access
Gender and physical activity: understanding how gender norms and socialization affect activity choices and participation patterns
LGBTQ+ inclusion: creating affirming spaces for LGBTQ+ people in physical activity programs
Cultural diversity: respecting and incorporating diverse movement traditions, values, and approaches to wellness
Economic access: addressing cost barriers and creating affordable or free physical activity opportunities
Advocacy and policy change: understanding how to advocate for systemic changes that increase physical activity equity
